Earlier, you mentioned Supabase was an alternative to Firebase. How do they compare when it comes to the database?

While Firebase uses a NoSQL document store, Supabase gives you a full relational PostgreSQL database. This means you get all the power of SQL, joins, and complex queries, which are often much more flexible for scaling. Plus, you're not locked into a proprietary platform since Postgres is open-source.

How do I add more functionality like full-text search to my database?

Postgres is highly extensible, so you just need to enable the right extensions. For example, you can use the pg_cron extension to schedule recurring jobs or PostGIS for geo-queries. You can enable these with a single click in the Supabase dashboard.

What's the best way to handle row-level security in my new app?

You should use Row Level Security (RLS) policies to control access on a row-by-row basis. By enabling RLS on your tables, you can ensure that users only interact with data they actually own. It's the gold standard for keeping your backend secure without writing complex middleware.

Can you list three social auth providers Supabase supports?

Sure thing! We support popular options like Google, GitHub, and Discord, among many others. You can also integrate more specialized providers like Spotify or Slack depending on your app's needs. It makes onboarding a lot smoother for your users.

What's the purpose of Supabase Storage?

Storage is designed to handle large files like images or videos that don't belong inside a database table. It's fully integrated with your Postgres database, meaning you can use RLS policies to control who can view or upload files. It makes serving media a breeze while keeping everything secure.
